evals.prompt: /build-fix The TypeScript build fails after my last change. Fix it with minimal diffs and rerun the relevant command.
evals.expected_output: Detects the TypeScript build command, groups errors, fixes one root cause at a time, reruns validation, and reports original/final error counts, fixed/remaining errors, and new errors introduced.
assertions: Detects the build system and names the exact command used or selected.
Groups errors by root cause before making fixes.
Fixes errors incrementally and reruns relevant validation after each meaningful fix.
Reports original and final error counts, remaining errors, and whether new errors were introduced.
Does not install new dependencies or make broad architecture changes without stopping to report the blocker.
expectations: Detects the build system and names the exact command used or selected.

Fixes errors incrementally and reruns relevant validation after each meaningful fix.
Reports original and final error counts, remaining errors, and whether new errors were introduced.
Does not install new dependencies or make broad architecture changes without stopping to report the blocker.

evals.prompt: cargo build is failing with import and trait errors. Work through the build safely.
evals.expected_output: Runs or uses cargo output, fixes syntax/import/type issues incrementally, and stops before broad architecture changes.
assertions: Detects the build system and names the exact command used or selected.
Groups errors by root cause before making fixes.
Fixes errors incrementally and reruns relevant validation after each meaningful fix.
Reports original and final error counts, remaining errors, and whether new errors were introduced.
Does not install new dependencies or make broad architecture changes without stopping to report the blocker.

Does not install new dependencies or make broad architecture changes without stopping to report the blocker.

evals.prompt: CI says a module is missing. Please fix the build, but don't install new packages without telling me.
evals.expected_output: Checks whether the dependency exists, suggests install if required, and does not silently add packages.
assertions: Detects the build system and names the exact command used or selected.
Groups errors by root cause before making fixes.
Fixes errors incrementally and reruns relevant validation after each meaningful fix.
Reports original and final error counts, remaining errors, and whether new errors were introduced.
Does not install new dependencies or make broad architecture changes without stopping to report the blocker.
